blocks:
Chapter Al-Qalam (The Pen)

Makkan, and it consists of 52 verses

(١) ن وَالْقَلَمِ وَما يَسْطُرُونَ. (1) Nūn. By the pen and what they write,

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Sufyan, from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "As for ن ** (Nūn), it is a river in Paradise. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Freeze!' So it froze and became ink (مداداً). Then He, the Mighty and Majestic, said to the Pen (الْقَلَمِ): 'Write!' So the Pen (الْقَلَمِ) inscribed upon the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ) what has been and what will be until the Day of Resurrection. The ink (المداد) is ink of light, and الْقَلَمِ ** is a pen of light, and the Tablet (اللّوح) is a tablet of light." Sufyan said: "So I said to him: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, explain to me the matter of the Tablet (اللّوح), the Pen (الْقَلَمِ), and the Ink (المداد) with further clarification, and teach me from what Allah has taught You.'" He replied: "O son of Sa'id, were you not worthy of the answer, I would not have answered you. The Nūn (نون) is an angel who conveys [information] to the Pen (الْقَلَمِ), and it is an angel. The Pen (الْقَلَمِ) conveys [information] to the Tablet (اللّوح), and it is an angel. The Tablet (اللّوح) conveys [information] to Israfil, and Israfil conveys [information] to Mika'il, and Mika'il conveys [information] to Jibra'il, and Jibra'il conveys [information] to the Prophets and Messengers (may the blessings of Allah be upon them)." He said: "Then he said to me: 'Arise, O Sufyan, for I do not feel safe for you.'"

In al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "As for ن ** (Nūn), it was a river in Paradise, whiter than snow and sweeter than honey. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to it: 'Be ink (مداداً).' Then He took a tree and planted it with His Hand (بيده)—and He said: 'The Hand (اليد) means Power (القوّة), and not in the sense that the anthropomorphists (المشبّهة) assume'—then He said to it: 'Be a pen (قلماً).' Then He said to it: 'Write!' So it said to Him: 'O Lord, what should I write?' He said: 'What will be until the Day of Resurrection.' So it did that, then He sealed it and said: 'You shall not speak until the Day of the Appointed Time (الوقت المعلوم).'"

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "The first thing Allah created was **الْقَلَمِ ** (the Pen), and He said to it: 'Write!' So it wrote what has been and what will be until the Day of Resurrection."

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "ن ** (Nūn) is a river in Paradise. Allah said to it: 'Be ink (مداداً).' So it froze, and it was whiter than milk and sweeter than honey. Then He said to the Pen (الْقَلَمِ): 'Write!' So the Pen (الْقَلَمِ) wrote what has been and what will be until the Day of Resurrection. Another narrative concerning this meaning has already passed in Surat Al-Jathiyah."

In al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله has ten names: five in the Quran and five that are not in the Quran. They are Muhammad, Ahmad, 'Abdullah, Yasin (يس), and Nūn (ن)." صلى الله عليه وآله.

(٢) ما أَنْتَ بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ بِمَجْنُونٍ ** (2) You are not, by your Lord’s blessing, a lunatic. This is the answer to the oath (جواب القسم), meaning: You are not a lunatic, since He has bestowed upon you the blessing of prophethood (النبوّة) and soundness of judgment (حصافة الرّأي). This is a response to their saying: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِي نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الذِّكْرُ إِنَّكَ لَمَجْنُونٌ (O you to whom the Reminder has been sent down! You are indeed a lunatic!).

(٣) وَإِنَّ لَكَ ** (3) and indeed there is for you an unfailing reward. (Due to your bearing the burdens of the Message (الرّسالة) and fulfilling its obligations (مواجبها)) an unfailing reward (لَأَجْراً غَيْرَ مَمْنُونٍ), meaning: a reward (لثواباً) that is not cut off (غير مقطوع) or one that is not held as a favor (غير ممنون) against you.

(٤)** وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلى خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍ ** (4) and indeed you possess a great disposition. Because you endure from your people what no one else endures.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, disciplined His Prophet and perfected His discipline of him. When He completed the discipline for him, He said: إِنَّكَ لَعَلى خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍ (and indeed you possess a great disposition). And in a narration: He disciplined His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم according to His love for him.

And in al-Basa'ir, narrated in an interrupted chain (مقطوعاً): Indeed, Allah disciplined His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and perfected His discipline of him, and He said: خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجاهِلِينَ (Take to pardon, command what is right, and turn away from the ignorant). When that was the case, Allah revealed: إِنَّكَ لَعَلى خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍ (and indeed you possess a great disposition).

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says (it means) upon a great religion (دين عظيم). And similarly in al-Ma'ani, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is Islam (الإسلام).

(٥) فَسَتُبْصِرُ وَيُبْصِرُونَ. (5) Soon you will see, and they will see, (٦) بِأَيِّكُمُ الْمَفْتُونُ ** (6) which of you is the afflicted one. Meaning: Which of you is the one afflicted (فُتِنَ) with madness (الجنون), and the باء (Bāʾ) is extra (مزيدة); or, with which of you is the madness (الجنون), based on the premise that المفتون is a verbal noun (مصدراً); and which of you is more deserving (أحرى) of this name (الاسم), you or them? In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "There is no believer except that My love (ودّي) has reached his heart, and My love has not reached the heart of anyone except that the love of Ali (ودّ عليّ) has reached his heart. O Ali, whoever claims that he loves Me and hates you has lied." He (peace be upon him) said: Then two men from the hypocrites (المنافقين) said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has certainly been afflicted (فُتِنَ) by this youth (الغلام)." So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed: فَسَتُبْصِرُ وَيُبْصِرُونَ بِأَيِّكُمُ الْمَفْتُونُ (Soon you will see, and they will see, which of you is the afflicted one). He said: It was revealed concerning those two, up to the end of the verses. And it was said that it was revealed concerning al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah. He used to prevent his clan (عشيرته) from accepting Islam. He was wealthy (موسراً) and had ten sons. He used to say to them and to his relatives (لحمته): "Whoever among you embraces Islam, I will withhold my aid (رفدي) from him." He was also an adopted son (دعيّاً), whom his father claimed after eighteen years from his birth. Thus is it mentioned in al-Jawamiʿ.

(٧) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ ضَلَّ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ. (7) Your Lord is indeed the best knower of those who stray from His way, and He is the best knower of the guided. (٨) فَلا تُطِعِ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (8) So do not obey the deniers. (٩) وَدُّوا لَوْ تُدْهِنُ فَيُدْهِنُونَ ** (9) They wish you would compromise so that they would compromise. [Meaning] you would soften towards them, so they would soften towards you. Al-Qummi said: That is, they wished that you would deceive (تغشّ) regarding Ali (peace be upon him), so they would deceive (يغشون) with you. (١٠) وَلا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ ** (10) And do not obey any contemptible habitual swearer, [Meaning] one who swears much (كثير الحلف). مَهِينٍ ** [meaning] despicable in opinion (حقير الرّأي). (١١) هَمَّازٍ ** (11) slanderer, going about talebearing, [Meaning] one who finds fault (عيّاب) and backbites (طعّان). مَشَّاءٍ بِنَمِيمٍ ** [meaning] one who transmits speech (نقّال للحديث) for the purpose of malicious gossip (على وجه السّعاية). (١٢) مَنَّاعٍ لِلْخَيْرِ ** (12) hinderer of good, transgressor, sinful, [Meaning] he prevents people from good, such as faith (الإيمان), spending (الإنفاق), and righteous deeds (العمل الصالح). مُعْتَدٍ [meaning] one who exceeds limits in injustice (متجاوز في الظلم). أَثِيمٍ ** [meaning] one who commits many sins (كثير الآثام). (١٣) عُتُلٍ ** (13) coarse, and on top of all that, base-born. [Meaning] rough (جاف) and harsh (غليظ). بَعْدَ ذلِكَ ** [meaning] after what has been enumerated of his faults (مثالبه). زَنِيمٍ.**

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: عُتُلٍّ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ زَنِيمٍ ** (coarse, and on top of all that, base-born). He replied: The al-ʿutull (العتلّ) is the one whose disbelief (الكفر) is immense, and the al-zanīm (الزّنيم) is the one who is reckless (المستهتر) in his disbelief.

In al-Majma': The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked about the al-ʿutull (العتلّ) and the al-zanīm (الزنيم). He said: "He is the one who is strong in creation (الشديد الخلق), healthy (المصحاح), a great eater (الأكول), a great drinker (الشّروب), one who finds food and drink (الواجد للطّعام والشراب), unjust to people (الظلوم للنّاس), and wide in the belly (الرّحب الجوف)." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "No jawwāẓ (جوّاظ), nor jaʿẓarī (جعظريّ), nor ʿutull zanīm (عتلّ زنيم) shall enter Paradise." He was asked: "What is the al-jawwāẓ (الجوّاظ)?" He said: "Every collector (جمّاع) and hinderer (منّاع)." He was asked: "What is the al-jaʿẓarī (الجعظريّ)?" He said: "The rude (الفظّ) and harsh (الغليظ)." He was asked: "What is the al-ʿutull al-zanīm (العتلّ الزّنيم)?" He said: "Every wide-bellied (رحب الجوف), ill-mannered (سيّء الخلق), great eater (أكول), great drinker (شروب), oppressive (غشوم), and unjust (ظلوم) person."

And narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): The al-zanīm (الزّنيم) is he who has no origin (لا أصل له). Al-Qummi said: The habitual swearer (الحلّاف) is the second one [Caliph] who swore to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he would not break a covenant. هَمَّازٍ مَشَّاءٍ بِنَمِيمٍ ** (slanderer, going about talebearing). He said: He used to gossip (ينمّ) about the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and slander (يهمز) among his companions. مَنَّاعٍ لِلْخَيْرِ ** (hinderer of good). He said: The good (الخير) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). مُعْتَدٍ ** (transgressor). He said: That is, he transgressed against him [Ali]. عُتُلٍّ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ زَنِيمٍ ** (coarse, and on top of all that, base-born). He said: The al-ʿutull (العتلّ) is the one whose disbelief (الكفر) is immense, and the al-zanīm (الزنيم) is the claimant (الدّعيّ).

(١٤) أَنْ كانَ ذا مالٍ وَبَنِينَ ** (14) If he was a man of wealth and sons. Because he was wealthy (متموّلاً) and supported (مستظهراً) by sons (بالبنين). And this is either related to [the previous phrase] 'do not obey' (لا تطع) or to what comes after it. And 'ان كان' (أَنْ كَانَ) was recited as an interrogative (على الاستفهام). (١٥) إِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِ آياتُنا قالَ أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (15) When Our signs are recited to him, he says, 'Myths of the ancients!' Meaning, their lies (اكاذيبهم). He said this out of excessive arrogance (من فرط غروره). (١٦) سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الْخُرْطُومِ ** (16) Soon We shall brand him on the snout. [Meaning] on the nose (على الأنف). It is said that a wound (جراحة) struck the nose (انف) of Al-Walid (الوليد) on the Day of Badr, and its trace (اثره) remained. And it is said that it is a metaphor (كناية) for Him humiliating him (يذله) with the utmost humiliation (غاية الاذلال), like their saying: 'He cut off his nose' (جدع انفه) and 'May his nose be rubbed in the dust' (رغم انفه). And Al-Qummi said regarding إِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِ (When they are recited to him): He used a metaphor (كنّى) for the second [person]. قالَ أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ (he says, 'Myths of the ancients!'): Meaning, the lies (أكاذيب) of the ancients. Regarding سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الْخُرْطُومِ (Soon We shall brand him on the snout), he said: [This occurs] in the Return (الرجعة), when the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) returns, and his enemies return, and He brands them (فيسمهم) with a branding iron (بميسم) that is with Him, just as livestock (البهائم) are branded on the snouts (الخراطيم), which are the nose (الانف) and the two lips (والشفتان). I say: And its explanation (بيانه) has already passed in the exegesis (تفسير) of دَابَّةُ الْأَرْضِ (the creature of the earth) in Surat al-Nahl.

(١٧) ** إِنَّا بَلَوْناهُمْ (17) We have certainly tested them) We tested the people of Mecca with drought (القحط) كَما بَلَوْنا أَصْحابَ الْجَنَّةِ ** (just as We tested the owners of the garden) the owners of the orchard (البستان) which was near (بدون) Sana'a (صنعاء).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the people of Mecca were afflicted with hunger (الجوع) just as the owners of the garden (الجنّة) were afflicted. And it was a garden (جنّة) that existed in the world (الدنيا) and was in Yemen, called al-Ridwan (الرضوان), nine miles from Sana'a (صنعاء). إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا لَيَصْرِمُنَّها مُصْبِحِينَ ** (when they swore that they would certainly harvest it in the morning) they would certainly cut it (the fruit) at the time of morning (وقت الصباح).

(١٨) وَلا يَسْتَثْنُونَ And they did not say إن شاء الله (if Allah wills). And it was only named استثناء (exception/stipulation) because of the exclusion (الإخراج) contained within it. (18) And they did not make any exception. (١٩) فَطافَ عَلَيْها upon the garden (الجنّة) طائِفٌ a sweeping affliction (بلاء طائف) مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَهُمْ نائِمُونَ. (19) There came upon it a visitation from your Lord while they were asleep. (٢٠) فَأَصْبَحَتْ كَالصَّرِيمِ It was said like the orchard whose fruits have been harvested (صرم) such that nothing remains in it, or like the dark night (الليل المظلم) due to its burning and blackening, or like the day (النّهار) due to its whiteness from excessive dryness (فرط اليبس). And الصّريمان (the two صريم) are the night (اللّيل) and the day (النهار) because of the cutting off (انصرام) of one from the other. (20) So it became like a harvested field.

(٢١) فَتَنادَوْا مُصْبِحِينَ. (21) Then they called out to one another in the morning, (٢٢) أَنِ اغْدُوا عَلى حَرْثِكُمْ ** (22) ‘Go early to your tillage Go out to it early in the morning (غدوة). [The verb اغْدُوا] implies the meaning of approaching (الإقبال) or taking possession (الاستيلاء), hence it is followed by the preposition على. ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صارِمِينَ ** if you are going to harvest.’ [Meaning] cutting it off (قاطعين له). (٢٣) فَانْطَلَقُوا وَهُمْ يَتَخافَتُونَ ** (23) So they set out, whispering to one another, They were speaking secretly (يتسارّون) among themselves. (٢٤) أَنْ لا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا الْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِسْكِينٌ. (24) ‘that no needy person should enter it today against you.’ (٢٥) وَغَدَوْا عَلى حَرْدٍ قادِرِينَ ** (25) They went early, determined to withhold, [Meaning] capable of spite (على نكد قادرين), and nothing else, instead of their ability to benefit [from the garden]. This means that they resolved to inflict hardship (يتنكّدوا) upon the needy, so hardship (تنكّد) was inflicted upon them, such that they were only capable of spite (النّكد) and deprivation (الحرمان) therein. (٢٦) فَلَمَّا رَأَوْها ** (26) When they saw it, The very first time they saw it, ** قالُوا إِنَّا لَضَالُّونَ ** they said, ‘We have certainly lost our way! We have mistaken the path to our garden, and this is not it. (٢٧) بَلْ نَحْنُ مَحْرُومُونَ ** (27) Rather we are deprived!’ That is, after they reflected and realized that it was indeed their garden, they said: We have been deprived (حرمنا) of its goodness due to our transgression (لجنايتنا) against ourselves. (٢٨) قالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ ** (28) The best of them said, The best (خيرهم) of them [said], ** أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكُمْ لَوْ لا تُسَبِّحُونَ ** ‘Did I not tell you, “Why do you not glorify [Allah]?”’ Why do you not remember Allah and thank Him by fulfilling His right, and repent to Him concerning your intention (نيّتكم)? (٢٩) قالُوا سُبْحانَ رَبِّنا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ. (29) They said, ‘Immaculate is our Lord! We have indeed been wrongdoers!’ (٣٠) فَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ يَتَلاوَمُونَ ** (30) Then some of them advanced toward others, reproaching one another. Some of them were blaming others (يلوم بعضهم بعضاً), for among them were those who suggested that [plan], and among them were those who approved of it (استصوبه), and among them were those who remained silent, being content [with it] (راضياً), and among them were those who denounced it (أنكره).
(٣١)** قالُوا يا وَيْلَنا إِنَّا كُنَّا طاغِينَ ** (31) They said, 'Woe to us! We have indeed been rebellious!' transgressing the limits of Allah.

(٣٢) عَسى رَبُّنا أَنْ يُبْدِلَنا خَيْراً مِنْها ** (32) Maybe our Lord will give us a better substitute than it. by the blessing of repentance and the admission of sin. It has been narrated that they were given a better substitute than it. إِنَّا إِلى رَبِّنا راغِبُونَ ** Indeed, we are supplicants to our Lord. hoping for forgiveness, seeking goodness.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, a man commits a sin, and the provision (الرّزق) is averted from him." Then He recited this Ayah: إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا لَيَصْرِمُنَّها ** until His saying: وَهُمْ نائِمُونَ.**

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Ibn Abbas: It was said to him that a group among this Ummah claims that a servant may commit a sin and be deprived of provision (الرّزق) because of it. Ibn Abbas said: "By Him besides Whom there is no god, this has a light in the Book of Allah brighter than the shining sun!" Allah mentioned in Surat Nun and al-Qalam that an old man had a garden (جنّة), and he would not bring any fruit from it into his house or dwelling until he had given every rightful person his due. When the old man passed away, his sons inherited him. He had five sons. His garden bore fruit that year—the year their father perished—a yield it had never borne before. The young men went to their garden after the Asr prayer and saw abundant fruit and surplus provision (رزق فاضل) the like of which they had not witnessed during their father's life. When they looked at the surplus, they transgressed (طغوا) and acted wrongfully (بغوا). Some of them said to others: "Our father was an old man whose mind had gone and who had become senile. Come, let us make a covenant among ourselves that we will not give anything to any of the poor Muslims this year until we become rich and our wealth increases. Then we will resume the practice (الصنعة) in the coming years." Four of them agreed to this, but the fifth was displeased. He is the one about Whom Allah said: قالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكُمْ لَوْ لا تُسَبِّحُونَ (28) The best among them said, 'Did I not tell you, "Why do you not glorify [Allah]?"'

It was asked: "O Ibn Abbas, was he the middle one in age?" He replied: "No, rather he was the youngest of the people in age, but the greatest of them in intellect (عقلاً). The middle (أوسط) of the people is the best of the people." Allah said: وَكَذلِكَ جَعَلْناكُمْ أُمَّةً وَسَطاً ** (143) Thus We have made you a middle nation, So the best among them said to them: "Fear Allah and follow the path (منهاج) of your father, and you will be safe and gain spoils." But they attacked him and beat him severely (ضرباً مبرماً). When the brother realized that they intended to kill him, he reluctantly (كارهاً لأمرهم) and unwillingly joined their consultation. They returned to their homes, then swore by Allah that they would harvest (يصرموا) when they awoke, and they did not say إن شاء الله (if Allah wills). So Allah afflicted them with that sin and intervened between them and that provision (الرزق) which they had anticipated. He informed about them in the Book and said: إِنَّا بَلَوْناهُمْ كَما بَلَوْنا أَصْحابَ الْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا لَيَصْرِمُنَّها مُصْبِحِينَ (17) We have indeed tested them just as We tested the owners of the garden, when they swore that they would surely harvest it in the morning, وَلا يَسْتَثْنُونَ فَطافَ عَلَيْها طائِفٌ مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَهُمْ نائِمُونَ فَأَصْبَحَتْ كَالصَّرِيمِ ** (18) and did not make any exception. (19) Then a visitation from your Lord came upon it while they slept. (20) So it became like a harvested field. He said: Like the burnt (كالمحترق). It was asked of Ibn Abbas: "What is al-Sarim (الصّريم)?" He said: "The dark night (اللّيل المظلم)." Then he said: "There is no light (ضوء) or illumination (نور) in it." When the people awoke in the morning, فَتَنادَوْا مُصْبِحِينَ أَنِ اغْدُوا عَلى حَرْثِكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صارِمِينَ ** (21) So they called out to one another in the morning, (22) [saying,] 'Go early to your tillage, if you are going to harvest.' He said: فَانْطَلَقُوا وَهُمْ يَتَخافَتُونَ ** (23) So they set out, whispering to one another, It was asked: "What is al-takhafutt (التخافت), O Ibn Abbas?" He said: "They were whispering secrets (يتسارّون), one whispering to another, so that no one besides them would hear." So they said: أَنْ لا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا الْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِسْكِينٌ وَغَدَوْا عَلى حَرْدٍ قادِرِينَ ** (24) 'No needy person shall enter it today against you.' (25) They went early, determined to prevent [them]. And in their souls, they intended to harvest it, not knowing what had befallen them from the might (سطوات) and vengeance (نقمته) of Allah. فَلَمَّا رَأَوْها وعاينوا ما قد حلّ بهم قالُوا إِنَّا لَضَالُّونَ بَلْ نَحْنُ مَحْرُومُونَ (26) But when they saw it, they said, 'We have surely gone astray! (27) Rather we are deprived.' So Allah deprived them of that provision (الرزق) because of a sin that originated from them, and He did not wrong them in the least.

(٣٣)** كَذلِكَ الْعَذابُ ** (33) Such is the punishment. [It is] like the punishment with which We afflicted the people of Mecca and the owners of the garden in this world. ** وَلَعَذابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ** But the punishment of the Hereafter is surely greater. [It is] mightier than it. ** لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ ** If they but knew. They would have guarded themselves against that which leads them to the punishment.

(٣٤) إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ ** (34) Indeed, for the Godwary there will be gardens of bliss near their Lord. Gardens in which there is nothing except pure bliss (التنعّم الخالص).

(٣٥)** أَفَنَجْعَلُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ كَالْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (35) Shall We treat the Muslims like the guilty?

This is a rejection of their saying (the disbelievers), [which was]: "If it is true that we shall be resurrected, as Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and those with him claim, they will not be superior to us; rather, we will be in a better state than them, just as we are in the world."

** ما لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ ** (36) (What is the matter with you? How do you judge?) A stylistic shift (التفات) is present in this [Ayah], containing astonishment at their judgment and deeming it far-fetched, and indicating that it originates from a corruption of thought (اختلال فكر) and a deviation of opinion (اعوجاج رأي).
(37) ** أَمْ لَكُمْ كِتابٌ ** from the heaven ** فِيهِ تَدْرُسُونَ ** you read (تقرءون). (37) Or do you have a Book in which you study?

(٣٨) ** إِنَّ لَكُمْ فِيهِ لَما تَخَيَّرُونَ ** (38) you shall indeed have in it whatever you choose. Indeed, for you is what you choose (تختارونه) and desire (تشتهونه). It is said: تخيّر the thing (الشيء) and اختارَه (chose it) means he took its best (خيره). And إنّ is vocalized with a kasrah due to the position of the لَام (of emphasis), and it allows for the possibility of being a new sentence (الاستيناف).

(٣٩) أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمانٌ عَلَيْنا ** (39) Or do you have oaths binding Us [Meaning] covenants confirmed by oaths (بالأيمان), reaching (بالِغَةٌ) the utmost limit in confirmation (في التوكيد). ** إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ ** until the Day of Resurrection, established for you against Us until the Day of Resurrection, [oaths] from the obligation (عهدته) of which He does not depart until We judge between you on that Day. ** إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَما تَحْكُمُونَ ** that you shall indeed have whatever you judge? This is the answer (جواب) to the oath (القسم) implied in ** أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمانٌ.
(٤٠) سَلْهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ بِذلِكَ زَعِيمٌ ** (Ask them which of them is surety for that.) A guarantor (كفيل) for that judgment (الحكم), claiming it and validating it.

(٤١) أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكاءُ ** (41) Do they have partners? (The partners are those) whom they make in the Hereafter like the believers, or (the partners) share with them in this saying. So, they imitate them, since there is nothing less than imitation. ** فَلْيَأْتُوا بِشُرَكائِهِمْ إِنْ كانُوا صادِقِينَ ** Then let them bring their partners, if they are truthful. in their claim (دعواهم).
(٤٢) يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَنْ ساقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى السُّجُودِ فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ. (42) The day when the shank is bared, and they are summoned to prostrate themselves, but they will not be able to.

(٤٣) خاشِعَةً أَبْصارُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ** (with downcast eyes, ignominy overwhelming them.)

[This occurs] on the Day when the matter intensifies and the affair becomes difficult. And the phrase "the uncovering of the leg (كشف السّاق)" is a proverb (مثل) regarding that. Its origin is the rolling up of the garments of those who are fearful from their legs (سوقهنّ) during flight, or [it means] the Day when the origin (أصل) of the matter and its reality (حقيقته) are uncovered, such that it becomes manifest (عياناً). [This phrase is] borrowed from the trunk (ساق) of a tree and the leg (ساق) of a human. Its indefiniteness (تَنكيره) is for the purpose of terrifying (التّهويل) or magnifying (التعظيم).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): They both said regarding this Ayah: The people were silenced, and awe (الهيبة) entered them, and the eyes stared fixedly (شخصت الأبصار), and the hearts reached the throats (الحناجر) due to the regret (الندامة), disgrace (الخزي), and ignominy (الذلّة) that overwhelmed them. And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): the like of it.

And therein [in al-Tawhid], and in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: A veil (حجاب) of light will be uncovered, and the believers will fall down prostrating (سجّداً), but the backs (أصلاب) of the hypocrites will be stiffened (يُدَبِّخُ). فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (so they will not be able) to prostrate (السّجود). And in al-Majma', in the narration: The backs (ظهور) of the hypocrites will become like skewers (كالسفافيد). And in al-Jawami', in the Hadith: Their backs (أصلابهم) will remain a single layer (طبقاً واحداً), meaning a single vertebra (فقارة واحدة) that does not bend. وَقَدْ كانُوا يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى السُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سالِمُونَ (though they were summoned to prostrate while they were sound).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): وَهُمْ سالِمُونَ (while they were sound) means: capable (مستطيعون), able to take what they were commanded with and leave what they were forbidden from. And for that reason, they were tested (ابتلوا). Then he said: There is nothing of what they were commanded with and forbidden from except that in it there is a trial (ابتلاء) and a decree (قضاء) from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ).

It was said: And in it is a threat (وعيد) for whoever hears the call to prayer (النداء الى الصلاة) but does not respond and sits back from the congregation (الجماعة). And al-Qummi said: [The uncovering] is about the matters that were hidden, and what they usurped from the rights (حقّهم) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family). وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى السُّجُودِ (and they will be summoned to prostrate). He said: [The veil] will be uncovered for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and their necks (أعناقهم) will become like the horns (صياصي) of cattle, meaning their horns (قرونها). فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (so they will not be able) to prostrate. And this is a punishment (عقوبة) because they did not obey Allah in the world regarding His command, which is His saying: وَقَدْ كانُوا يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى السُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سالِمُونَ (though they were summoned to prostrate while they were sound). He said: [They were summoned] to His Wilayah (ولايته) in the world while they were capable (يستطيعون).

(٤٤) ** فَذَرْنِي وَمَنْ يُكَذِّبُ بِهذَا الْحَدِيثِ ** (44) So leave Me and him who denies this discourse. Leave all of him to Me, for I will suffice you against him. سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُمْ ** We will bring them near to the punishment, degree by degree, through granting respite, the continuation of health, the increase of blessing, and causing them to forget remembrance (الذكر), مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَعْلَمُونَ that it is a gradual drawing near (استدراج).

(٤٥) وَأُمْلِي لَهُمْ ** (45) And I respite them and I grant them respite (أُمْهِلُهُمْ). ** إِنَّ كَيْدِي مَتِينٌ ** My stratagem is indeed firm cannot be repelled by anything. He named it a stratagem (كَيْدًا) because it is in its form (صُورَتِهِ). And the explanation of gradual enticement (الاستدراج) and the exegesis (تفسير) of the verse have already passed in Surat al-A'raf.

**(٤٦) أَمْ تَسْئَلُهُمْ أَجْراً ** (46) Or do you ask them for a reward (upon the guidance)

** فَهُمْ مِنْ مَغْرَمٍ ** (so they are weighed down by a debt) (from a fine (غرامة))

** مُثْقَلُونَ ** (heavily burdened) (by carrying it, so they turn away from you.)

**(٤٧) أَمْ عِنْدَهُمُ الْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ ** (47) Do they possess the Unseen, so they write it down? (From the Unseen) that which they decree, and by which they dispense with Your knowledge.

(٤٨) ** فَاصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ ** (48) So be patient for the judgement of your Lord, which is His granting them respite and delaying your victory over them, ** وَلا تَكُنْ كَصاحِبِ الْحُوتِ ** and do not be like the Man of the Fish (صاحب الحوت), meaning Jonah (يونس) when he prayed against his people, then departed in anger toward Allah, ** إِذْ نادى ** when he cried out in the belly of the fish, ** وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ. ** while he was choked with grief.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [It means] distressed (مغموم).

(٤٩) لَوْ لا أَنْ تَدارَكَهُ نِعْمَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (49) Had not a blessing from his Lord overtaken him The success (التوفيق) for repentance (للتوبة) and its acceptance (وقبولها). Al-Qummi said: The blessing (النعمة) is mercy (الرحمة). ** لَنُبِذَ بِالْعَراءِ ** he would have been cast on the bare ground (Meaning) the land (بالأرض) empty (الخالية) of trees (عن الأشجار) and roofs (والسقف). Al-Qummi said: The place (الموضع) which has no roof (لا سقف له). ** وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ ** while he was blameworthy. Reproached (مليم).

(٥٠) ** فَاجْتَباهُ رَبُّهُ ** (50) Thereupon his Lord chose him by returning the authority (الولاية) to him, فَجَعَلَهُ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ (and made him one of the righteous), meaning, among those who are perfect (الكاملين) in righteousness (الصلاح). And his story has already passed in its surah (سورته).

(٥١) وَإِنْ يَكادُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصارِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا الذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُ لَمَجْنُونٌ. (51) Indeed, the faithless almost trip you with their eyes when they hear the Reminder, and they say, 'He is indeed crazy.'

(52) ** وَما هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِلْعالَمِينَ ** (And it is nothing but a reminder for all the nations.) It means that due to the intensity of their enmity, the surge of their hatred, and their covenant (عهدهم) when hearing the Quran and the call to goodness, they look at you askance (شزراً) such that they almost make your foot slip (يزلون قدمك) and throw you down (فيصرعونك). This is derived from their saying: "He looked at me with a look that almost threw me down (يكاد يصرعني)," meaning: if it were possible for him to cause a fall (الصّرع) merely by a look, he would have done it.

In al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He passed by the Mosque of Ghadir and looked towards the left side (ميسرة) of the mosque and said: "That is the place where the foot of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله rested when He said: 'Of whomsoever I am the Mawla (مولاه), then Ali is his Mawla (مولاه).'" Then He looked towards the other side and said: "That is the place of the tent (فسطاط) of so-and-so (أبي فلان) and so-and-so (وفلان), and Salim, the Mawla (مولى) of Abu Hudhayfah, and Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah. When they saw Him raising His hand, some of them said to others: 'Look at his eyes, they are rolling as if they are the eyes of a madman.' Then Gabriel descended with this Ayah."

Al-Qummi said regarding لَمَّا سَمِعُوا الذِّكْرَ (when they heard the Reminder): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله informed them of the virtue of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), He (Allah) said: وَما هُوَ —meaning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)—**إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِلْعالَمِينَ ** (but a reminder for all the nations).

And it was said that the meaning is that they almost strike you with the evil eye (بالعين), as it was narrated: There were people in Banu Asad who were known for the evil eye (عيّانون), and some of them intended to strike him (the Prophet) with the evil eye (ان يعينه), so it was revealed. And in the Hadith: "Verily, the evil eye (العين) can cause a man to enter the grave and a camel to enter the pot (القدر)."

And in al-Majma', it came in the narration: Asma bint Umays said: "O Messenger of Allah, the children of Ja'far are afflicted by the evil eye (العين). Should I seek ruqyah (فأسترقي) for them?" He said: "Yes. For if anything could precede the Divine Decree (القدر), the evil eye (العين) would precede it." And it was read (وقرئ): ليزلقونك with a fathah on the ya (بفتح الياء).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat Nun and al-Qalam in an obligatory prayer (فريضة) or a supererogatory prayer (نافلة), Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will secure him from ever being afflicted by poverty (فقر), and Allah the Almighty will grant him refuge from the compression of the grave (ضمّة القبر) when he dies."
